input.background: The probability associated with a proposition can be used to quantify the confidence in a judgment. At one extreme, a probability statement of 0 represents the personal belief that the proposition can never happen. At the other extreme, a probability statement of 1 represents the personal belief that the proposition will always occur. Probabilities between these two extremes represent shades of gray.
input.question: Match the percentage 50% with the narrative statement that most closely reflects its meaning: This happens as often as not; chances are even.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

percentage.description: The percentage value representing the probability of an event occurring.

narrative_statement.value: This happens as often as not; chances are even.
narrative_statement.description: The narrative statement that corresponds to the given percentage probability.
